Ticketmaster.Events.Event.name: Event
Ticketmaster.Events.Event.description: A Ticketmaster event representing a concert, sports match, theater performance, or other live public event. Emitted when a new event is discovered or when an existing event's status or schedule changes.

Ticketmaster.Reference.Venue.name: Venue
Ticketmaster.Reference.Venue.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster venue. Emitted at bridge startup and refreshed periodically. Carries the stable venue identifier, location, address, timezone, and capacity information.

Ticketmaster.Reference.Attraction.name: Attraction
Ticketmaster.Reference.Attraction.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster attraction (performer, artist, sports team, or production). Emitted at bridge startup and refreshed periodically. Carries stable attraction identifier, name, and classification.

Ticketmaster.Reference.Classification.name: Classification
Ticketmaster.Reference.Classification.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster classification segment (e.g. Music, Sports, Arts & Theatre). Emitted at bridge startup and refreshed daily. Carries the stable segment identifier, name, and the primary genre and subgenre under that segment.

schema.name: Event
schema.doc: A Ticketmaster event representing a concert, sports match, theater performance, or other live public event.

fields.name: url
fields.doc: URL to the event page on Ticketmaster.com.

fields.name: locale
fields.doc: BCP-47 locale string for the event page.

fields.name: start_date
fields.doc: Local calendar date the event starts, YYYY-MM-DD.

fields.name: start_time
fields.doc: Local clock time the event starts, HH:MM:SS.

fields.name: start_datetime_local
fields.doc: Combined local start date and time as ISO 8601.

fields.name: start_datetime_utc
fields.doc: Start date and time in UTC as ISO 8601.

fields.name: status
fields.doc: On-sale status: onsale, offsale, cancelled, postponed, rescheduled.

fields.name: genre_id
fields.doc: Stable genre identifier within the segment.

fields.name: venue_city
fields.doc: City where the venue is located.

fields.name: currency
fields.doc: ISO 4217 currency code for price fields.

fields.name: onsale_start_datetime
fields.doc: UTC datetime when public ticket sales open.

fields.name: onsale_end_datetime
fields.doc: UTC datetime when public ticket sales close.

fields.name: info
fields.doc: General informational text about the event.

schema.name: Venue
schema.doc: Reference data for a Ticketmaster venue.

fields.name: url
fields.doc: URL to the venue page on Ticketmaster.com.

schema.name: Attraction
schema.doc: Reference data for a Ticketmaster attraction (performer, artist, sports team, or production).

fields.name: url
fields.doc: URL to the attraction page on Ticketmaster.com.

schema.name: Classification
schema.doc: Reference data for a Ticketmaster classification segment.

fields.name: primary_genre_id
fields.doc: Stable identifier for the primary genre within this segment.

fields.name: primary_subgenre_id
fields.doc: Stable identifier for the primary subgenre.

Event.name: Event
Event.description: A Ticketmaster event representing a concert, sports match, theater performance, or other live public event. Sourced from the Discovery API v2 /events endpoint.

event_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster event identifier. Globally unique across all event types (e.g. 'G5v0Z9iQkPl_2'). Used as the Kafka message key.

name.description: Human-readable event name as provided by Ticketmaster (e.g. 'Taylor Swift | The Eras Tour').

url.description: URL to the event page on Ticketmaster.com where tickets can be purchased.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale string for the event page (e.g. 'en-us', 'en-gb', 'de-de').

start_date.description: Local calendar date on which the event starts, in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format. May be absent for events without a confirmed date (time-to-be-announced).

start_time.description: Local clock time at which the event starts, in HH:MM:SS format. May be absent when the time is to be announced.

start_datetime_local.description: Combined local start date and time as an ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g. '2024-07-20T19:30:00'). Not adjusted to UTC.

start_datetime_utc.description: Start date and time expressed in UTC as an ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g. '2024-07-20T23:30:00Z'). Used as the CloudEvents time attribute.

status.description: Current on-sale status of the event. Known values: 'onsale' (tickets available), 'offsale' (tickets not available), 'cancelled' (event cancelled), 'postponed' (date changed, new date not yet set), 'rescheduled' (new date confirmed).

segment_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster classification segment identifier for this event (e.g. 'KZFzniwnSyZfZ7v7nJ' for Music). Corresponds to the top-level category.

segment_name.description: Human-readable name of the classification segment (e.g. 'Music', 'Sports', 'Arts & Theatre', 'Film', 'Miscellaneous').

genre_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster genre identifier within the segment. Second level of the classification hierarchy.

subgenre_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster subgenre identifier. Third level of the classification hierarchy, below genre.

venue_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster venue identifier for the primary venue where this event takes place. Matches entity_id in a Ticketmaster.Reference.Venue event.

venue_name.description: Human-readable name of the primary venue (e.g. 'Madison Square Garden').

venue_city.description: City where the venue is located.

venue_state_code.description: ISO 3166-2 state or province code for the venue location (e.g. 'NY', 'CA'). May be absent for non-US/CA venues.

venue_country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the venue location (e.g. 'US', 'GB', 'DE').

venue_latitude.description: WGS-84 latitude of the venue in decimal degrees. North is positive.

venue_longitude.description: WGS-84 longitude of the venue in decimal degrees. East is positive.

price_min.description: Minimum face-value ticket price in the currency indicated by the currency field. Provided by the price_ranges element in the Discovery API response when available.

price_max.description: Maximum face-value ticket price in the currency indicated by the currency field. Provided by the price_ranges element in the Discovery API response when available.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for price_min and price_max (e.g. 'USD', 'GBP', 'EUR').

attraction_ids.description: JSON-encoded array of Ticketmaster attraction identifiers associated with this event (e.g. '["K8vZ91718H7","K8vZ9179Ki7"]'). Each ID corresponds to an entity_id in a Ticketmaster.Reference.Attraction reference event.

attraction_names.description: JSON-encoded array of attraction names corresponding to the attraction_ids array (e.g. '["Taylor Swift","Sabrina Carpenter"]').

onsale_start_datetime.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime when public ticket sales open for this event.

onsale_end_datetime.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime when public ticket sales close for this event.

info.description: General informational text about the event as provided by the promoter or Ticketmaster. May include age restrictions, bag policies, or other event notes.

please_note.description: Important notes for ticket purchasers, such as bag-check policies, camera restrictions, or health and safety requirements. Displayed prominently on the event page.

Venue.name: Venue
Venue.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster venue. Carries the venue's stable identifier, location details, timezone, and geographic coordinates as returned by the Discovery API v2 /venues endpoint.

entity_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster venue identifier (e.g. 'KovZpaFVAeA'). Used as both the Kafka message key and the CloudEvents subject. Matches the venue_id field in Event records.

name.description: Human-readable name of the venue as listed on Ticketmaster (e.g. 'Madison Square Garden', 'The O2 Arena').

url.description: URL to the venue page on Ticketmaster.com.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale string for the venue page (e.g. 'en-us').

timezone.description: IANA timezone database name for the venue's local time (e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Used to interpret local start times in Event records.

city.description: City name where the venue is located.

state_code.description: ISO 3166-2 state or province code (e.g. 'NY', 'CA'). Populated for US and Canadian venues; absent for most international venues.

address.description: Street address line 1 for the venue (e.g. '4 Pennsylvania Plaza').

postal_code.description: Postal or ZIP code for the venue address.

latitude.description: WGS-84 latitude of the venue in decimal degrees. North is positive.

longitude.description: WGS-84 longitude of the venue in decimal degrees. East is positive.

Attraction.name: Attraction
Attraction.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster attraction, which represents a performer, musical artist, sports team, theatrical production, or other headline act. Sourced from the Discovery API v2 /attractions endpoint.

entity_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster attraction identifier (e.g. 'K8vZ91718H7'). Used as both the Kafka message key and the CloudEvents subject. Matches values in the attraction_ids array of Event records.

name.description: Human-readable name of the attraction as listed on Ticketmaster (e.g. 'Taylor Swift', 'Los Angeles Lakers').

url.description: URL to the attraction page on Ticketmaster.com.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale string for the attraction page (e.g. 'en-us').

segment_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster classification segment identifier for this attraction (e.g. 'KZFzniwnSyZfZ7v7nJ' for Music). Matches entity_id in Classification reference events.

segment_name.description: Human-readable name of the classification segment (e.g. 'Music', 'Sports', 'Arts & Theatre').

genre_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster genre identifier for this attraction within its segment.

subgenre_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster subgenre identifier for this attraction.

Classification.name: Classification
Classification.description: Reference data for a Ticketmaster classification segment, which represents the top level of the event classification hierarchy (Music, Sports, Arts & Theatre, Film, Miscellaneous, etc.). Emitted at bridge startup from the Discovery API v2 /classifications endpoint.

entity_id.description: Stable Ticketmaster segment identifier (e.g. 'KZFzniwnSyZfZ7v7nJ'). Used as both the Kafka message key and the CloudEvents subject. Matches segment_id in Event and Attraction records.

name.description: Human-readable name of the classification segment (e.g. 'Music', 'Sports', 'Arts & Theatre', 'Film', 'Miscellaneous').

type.description: Ticketmaster resource type discriminator for this classification entry. Usually 'segment'.

primary_genre_id.description: Stable identifier for the primary or most prominent genre within this segment. Provided when the API returns a primary_genre sub-object.

primary_genre_name.description: Human-readable name of the primary genre (e.g. 'Rock', 'Pop', 'Basketball').

primary_subgenre_id.description: Stable identifier for the primary subgenre under the primary genre.

primary_subgenre_name.description: Human-readable name of the primary subgenre.
